title: Fix voice greeting, command execution, mentor character speech, and PC control system

requirements.summary: Add automatic voice greeting after successful login using SpeechSynthesis API
acceptanceCriteria: After transition screen, SpeechSynthesis speaks 'Good [Morning/Afternoon/Evening], Welcome back, Master [username]' automatically
No user click or interaction is needed to trigger the voice greeting
The greeting uses the correct time-of-day prefix based on current hour
The username used is the logged-in user's name from their profile

file_operations.description: After the transition animation completes and before navigation to dashboard, invoke SpeechSynthesis to speak the greeting text. Use the timeGreeting utility to get the time-based prefix (Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening) and construct the spoken message as 'Good [time], Welcome back, Master [username]' where username comes from the user profile. The speech must trigger automatically without user interaction. Ensure the greeting is spoken even if the user has not yet interacted with the page by calling speechSynthesis.speak() in a useEffect that runs after the typing animation completes.

requirements.summary: Fix voice assistant command processing to parse and execute commands instead of echoing back the transcript
acceptanceCriteria: Saying 'open YouTube' (in any language, including Telugu transliteration) causes window.open('https://www.youtube.com', '_blank') to be called
Saying 'open [website name]' opens the corresponding URL in a new tab
The assistant never responds with 'Got it! You said: [transcript]' as a fallback — it must parse and act on the command
After executing an action, the assistant speaks a contextual confirmation (e.g., 'Opening YouTube now, sir.')
Command parsing handles Telugu-script wake word + command combinations (e.g., 'జార్విస్ ఓపెన్ youtube' correctly maps to open YouTube)
The command loop returns to wake-word listening after each action automatically

file_operations.description: Rewrite the command processing logic to parse user intent from the transcript instead of echoing it back. Implement intent detection for commands like 'open YouTube', 'open [URL/app name]', 'volume up/down', 'brightness up/down', 'scroll up/down', 'search Wikipedia for [topic]'. For 'open YouTube' or variants (including Telugu transliterations like 'ఓపెన్ youtube'), immediately call window.open('https://www.youtube.com', '_blank'). For other 'open' commands, map common app/site names to their URLs and call window.open. Remove any 'Got it! You said:' fallback responses. After executing a command, speak a contextual confirmation (e.g., 'Opening YouTube now, sir.') using the speak function. Ensure Telugu-script commands are normalized and mapped correctly. After each command execution and confirmation, return to wake-word listening state automatically.

requirements.summary: Rewrite mentor character dialogue to use authentic movie/anime character voice and style
acceptanceCriteria: Rengoku's motivational messages use his actual Demon Slayer catchphrases and speech style
Rengoku never says generic phrases like 'I'm here to help' — all dialogue is in-character
When the mentor card triggers a spoken message, SpeechSynthesis speaks it in Rengoku's authentic style
Other preset mentors (Naruto, Tony Stark) also have in-character authentic dialogue sets
The mentor's spoken responses during scheduling reminders and daily tips are fully in-character

file_operations.description: Replace all mentor dialogue entries with authentic character-specific messages. For Rengoku Kyojuro, use his actual Demon Slayer catchphrases and speech patterns such as 'SET YOUR HEART ABLAZE!', 'Umai! Umai! Umai!', 'My fire burns brighter than anyone!', and address the user as a fellow Hashira candidate or warrior. Remove any generic AI assistant phrases. For Naruto, use his authentic catchphrases like 'Believe it!', 'I never go back on my word!', references to ninja way and hokage dreams. For Tony Stark, use his Iron Man dialogue style with wit, sarcasm, and tech references. Ensure all motivational messages, schedule prompts, and reminders are fully in-character for each preset mentor.

When the mentor card displays or triggers a spoken message, ensure the text passed to SpeechSynthesis is fetched from the updated mentorMessages utility and is in the mentor's authentic voice. If the component has any logic that generates generic responses, replace it with calls to the character-specific message database. Verify that the spoken text matches the displayed text and is fully in-character.

requirements.summary: Implement real OS-level PC control for volume and brightness using web platform APIs with progressive enhancement and clear UI labeling
acceptanceCriteria: Volume up/down voice command uses Web Audio API GainNode on a persistent AudioContext to affect actual audio output level
Brightness control attempts window.screen.brightness API first; falls back to CSS overlay only if unavailable
The UI labels each PC control indicating whether it is 'OS Level' or 'App Overlay' so the user is not misled
Opening YouTube or any URL via voice command calls window.open(url, '_blank') which opens in the real OS browser
Voice commands for volume/brightness/open execute the OS-level action without requiring any UI button click
PCControlsPanel.tsx and usePCControls.ts are updated to use the best available OS-level API for each control

file_operations.description: Refactor the volume control to use the Web Audio API with a persistent AudioContext and GainNode to control actual audio output level. Create a single AudioContext on hook initialization (or resume an existing one) and connect a GainNode to the destination. When volume up/down is triggered, adjust the GainNode's gain value. For brightness control, attempt to use window.screen.brightness API first (if available on Chrome Android/desktop); if unsupported, fall back to the existing CSS opacity overlay method. Add a capability detection function that returns whether each control is operating at 'OS Level' or 'App Overlay'. Ensure the URL opening logic uses window.open(url, '_blank') as already implemented. Export the capability detection results so the UI can display accurate labels.

Update the UI to display a capability label next to each control (volume, brightness) indicating whether it is 'OS Level' or 'App Overlay' based on the detection results from usePCControls. For example, show a small badge or label like '[OS Level]' or '[App Overlay Only]' next to the volume and brightness controls. Ensure the user is clearly informed about the actual level of control being exercised. Update any help text or tooltips to explain the difference. No changes needed for the open URL control as it already uses window.open which opens in the OS browser.

Ensure that when volume/brightness/open commands are parsed and executed, they directly invoke the OS-level actions from usePCControls without requiring any UI button click. The command processing logic should call the volume/brightness control functions that use the Web Audio API or Screen Brightness API, and the open command should call window.open directly. Confirm that the assistant speaks a contextual confirmation after executing each OS-level action.
